title: Settlement Signature
description: The one memorable thing about a place. Templated so a handful of rows fan out into many thousands of distinct details.
entries: The whole place is built around one enormous old {pick:oak|yew|chestnut|elm|fig|banyan}, its {pick:roots|shade|sprawling limbs} {pick:buckling|cracking|swallowing|overshadowing} the {pick:market square|main road|old well|green|chapel steps}.

A {pick:crooked|leaning|squat|slender|windowless} {pick:clock tower|bell tower|watchtower|granary|lighthouse} that has been {pick:a little out of true|ten minutes slow|half-finished|scorched black} for as long as anyone remembers, and is quietly proud of it.
One {pick:paved|cobbled|planked} street runs through it; the rest are {pick:mud|dust|puddles|goose-churned mire}, and that one clean strip is fiercely proud of itself.
Every {pick:door|shutter|roof-tile|gatepost} is painted the same shade of {pick:blue|ochre|green|faded red|whitewash}, by {pick:a custom|an old law|a superstition} no one can quite explain.
A {pick:stone|timber|three-arched|humpbacked} bridge far grander than the place deserves, left by {pick:someone long gone|an emperor who never returned|a guild that went bust|the old road-builders}.
The bells ring at {pick:odd hours|dawn and dusk only|no fixed hour}, to a schedule only the {pick:old ringer|blind sexton|head priest} understands.
A {pick:weekly|monthly|dawn} market draws people from a surprising distance for one particular {pick:good|beast|craft|delicacy}.
The oldest building is a {pick:shrine|hall|tomb|watch-house} no one {pick:worships at|uses|visits} any more, kept {pick:spotless|locked|garlanded} all the same.
A local {pick:dish|cheese|brew|smoked fish} so {pick:pungent|potent|strange} that travellers {pick:smell|hear about|are warned of} the town before they see it.
The streets are unusually {pick:clean|tidy|swept}; the townsfolk are quietly, competitively {pick:houseproud|tidy|rivalrous about their gardens}.
A weathered {pick:statue|monument|carved pillar|iron figure} in the square whose {pick:subject|name|purpose} everyone has forgotten but no one will {pick:move|melt down|touch}.
{pick:Cats|Geese|Goats|Ravens|Dogs}. Far too many {pick:of them|for one town}, all of them plainly in charge.
A great {pick:waterwheel|windmill|forge-bellows|tannery} {pick:groans|creaks|thunders} day and night; the sound is so constant the locals no longer hear it.
The town keeps its clocks a {pick:quarter-hour|half-hour|full hour} {pick:fast|slow}, and swears the rest of the world is {pick:late|wrong}.
A single, improbably {pick:famous|skilled|eccentric} {pick:smith|weaver|brewer|clockmaker|luthier|glassblower} works here, and the town lives half in their shadow.
{pick:Washing|Netting|Drying herbs|Banners} strung across every lane overhead, so the streets are roofed in {pick:drying linen|shade|colour}.
An annual {pick:festival|contest|procession} the town takes far too seriously, involving a {pick:goose|greased pole|barrel of ale|painted boat} and a great deal of shouting.
The {pick:well|fountain|spring} in the square is said to {pick:grant a wish|cure warts|show your true love|never run dry}; it mostly grants cold, clean water.
Terraced {pick:gardens|vines|orchards} climb every spare wall — the whole town is quietly, ferociously in {pick:bloom|fruit|leaf}.
One family has held the same {pick:feud|lawsuit|grudge} with another for {pick:six|four|nine} generations; no one remembers why.
A crumbling town wall now used mostly for {pick:drying fish|drying laundry|courting couples|grazing goats}.
The {pick:inn|tavern|alehouse} is older than the town and insists, loudly, on being {pick:addressed as such|the first stop for any traveller}.
Everything smells faintly of {pick:woodsmoke|salt|tanning|baking bread|peat|pine}.
A row of ancient {pick:standing stones|boundary markers|carved posts} the town has simply built around and named after {pick:taverns|saints|dead mayors}.
The locals speak with an accent so {pick:thick|clipped|singsong} that {pick:neighbouring villages|travellers|even the priest} need a moment to follow.
A painted line on the road marks where {pick:a saint once rested|a duel was fought|the plague stopped|a miracle happened}; carts still swerve to avoid it.
The market never closes — there is always someone, at any hour, selling {pick:something|fish|charms|hot food} to someone.
The roofs split hard between {pick:turf and slate|thatch and red tile|shingle and lead|reed and grey stone}, in two stubborn, opposed halves of town.
A tame {pick:raven|cat|magpie|old hound} has the run of the place and is widely believed to {pick:understand more than it lets on|bring luck|be the reincarnated founder}.
The town is proud of a {pick:battle|flood|siege|fire} it once {pick:lost|barely survived|caused itself}, and tells the story better every year.
Bridges everywhere — the place is stitched together over a dozen little {pick:channels|streams|canals|mill-races}.
A single {pick:lamp|candle|lantern} is kept burning in the {pick:tallest window|shrine|harbour tower}, for {pick:a ship long overdue|a person who never came home|the dead|luck}.
A {pick:hot spring|deep well|cold cistern} feeds the {pick:public baths|town fountain|brewery}; the whole town is {pick:cleaner|slower|healthier} for it.
The {pick:chapel bell|town horn|old gong} is cracked and sounds terrible, and the town would {pick:riot|weep|revolt} before it was replaced.
Vines have swallowed the old {pick:gatehouse|granary|watchtower} whole; in summer it is simply a {pick:green mound|thicket|hill}.
The town square doubles as a {pick:threshing floor|drill yard|fish market} by day and a {pick:dance floor|court|meeting ground} after.
A famous {pick:view|sunset|echo|overlook} — everyone who passes through is marched to the one spot to admire it.
The place {pick:brews|distils|smokes|pickles} something local and potent that it is quietly, dangerously proud of.
Every house keeps a small {pick:carved figure|painted charm|iron token} by the door, and no two are alike.
The main street bends hard around {pick:a boulder|an old oak|a shrine|a sinkhole} no one has ever managed to {pick:move|shift|be rid of}.
A famous {pick:crime|scandal|disappearance|swindle} happened here once, and the town has never fully lived it down.
The town runs on {pick:bartered favours|promissory chalk-marks|guild tokens} as much as coin, and outsiders find it baffling.
One {pick:long-dead hero|founding saint|drowned king} is buried under the {pick:crossroads|market cross|old bridge}, and travellers still leave a coin.
A colony of {pick:storks|swallows|bats|gulls} {pick:nests on the chimneys|roosts in the tower}; the town plans its year around their coming and going.
The place floods a little every {pick:spring|autumn} and simply lives above the waterline for a month.
Its walls are hung with faded {pick:guild banners|battle flags|trade signs} for {pick:trades|companies|families} that no longer exist here.
